note: Suspected correctness bug confirmed and fixed: the below(s,e,m) orientation test in the hull recursion used '< 0' (inverted sign), causing the divide-and-conquer to reject genuine lower-left hull vertices and stop early (e.g. n=4 returned 234 vs true optimum 231). Codex (write-enabled) flipped it to '> 0' in both reasoning.md and answer.md, added a zero-direction guard in hull(), made the cross-product prose consistent, fixed the hull-vertex optimality argument (tangent/AM-GM), and hollowed context.md to a neutral pre-method scaffold (mst/solve stubs, '# TODO', no Background/Baselines/Evaluation). Re-verified independently against brute-force over all spanning trees: 83000 random small-graph comparisons (answer.md + reasoning.md code, incl. wide-weight dense and tie-heavy/parallel-edge stress) with ZERO disagreements, plus tree-consistency checks pass; the diagnosed counterexample now returns 231.
